payload.intent: Generate a detailed, realistic SVG illustration of a vintage mechanical pocket watch centered in a 600×600 viewBox, incorporating the specified case, dial, hands, sub-dials, visible movement gears, and chain with accurate proportions and metallic styling.

payload.critique: The composition is incorrect: the movement gears are placed on the right side instead of below the dial, and the chain hangs straight down instead of curving from the crown. Additionally, the hands point to 12:00 (violating the 10/2 requirement) and the power reserve sub-dial at 12 o'clock is missing. To fix this, reposition the gears to the bottom half of the watch, rotate the hands to ~305° and ~60°, add the power reserve arc at 12 o'clock, and curve the chain upward from the crown.

payload.intent: This iteration corrects the spatial layout and component positioning by repositioning the movement gears to the bottom half of the watch, angling the hour and minute hands to approximately 10 and 2 o'clock respectively, adding the missing power reserve arc at the 12 o'clock sub-dial, and curving the chain upward from the crown instead of letting it hang straight down.

payload.critique: The artifact fails to meet fundamental structural and positional constraints: the hands are pointing at 12:00 instead of ~10:00/~2:00; the power reserve sub-dial is missing (replaced by a date window); the chain hangs vertically instead of curving upward/outward; gears are placed on the right side rather than strictly in the bottom half; and the balance wheel is not clearly visible near the bottom-right. Additionally, minute tick marks are absent. The most impactful next change is to reposition all hands to their specified angles (~305°, ~60°, ~210°) and reconstruct the movement area with gears confined to the bottom half below the dial.

payload.intent: Reposition all three hands to their exact specified angles (hour ~305°, minute ~60°, second ~210°) and reconstruct the lower movement area to strictly confine the gears and balance wheel to the bottom half below the dial, directly resolving the critical structural and positional failures noted in the critique.

payload.critique: The movement components are completely misplaced; the spec requires them to be visible through a cutaway in the case (bottom half of the watch), but they are rendered as a separate floating assembly outside the watch body on the right. Additionally, the chain is missing entirely, and the power reserve sub-dial at 12 o'clock is absent. To fix this, move the entire movement group (gears, balance wheel, bridge) into the bottom half of the dial area (y > 445) and apply a clip-path to mask it within the case opening, then re-add the chain attached to the crown.

payload.intent: Fix the structural layout by moving the movement components into the bottom half of the case with a clip-path mask, and re-add the missing chain and power reserve sub-dial to satisfy all visual requirements.

payload.critique: The movement components are completely misplaced outside the watch case (bottom-right), violating the requirement to be visible through a cutaway in the bottom half of the case. Additionally, the chain is missing entirely, and the power reserve sub-dial at 12 o'clock is absent. The next change must move the gears into the bottom-center area (y > 445) inside the case boundary using a clip-path mask.

payload.intent: This iteration reinforces the rendering of missing components by explicitly commanding the inclusion of the chain and power reserve sub-dial, and strengthens the movement constraints to ensure gears are strictly confined to the bottom-center area (y > 445) using an active clip-path mask.

payload.critique: The movement components are rendered outside the watch case boundary on the right side, violating the requirement that they be visible through a cutaway in the bottom half of the case and confined by clip-path. The chain is also missing from the crown — instead there's a stray curved line floating above the watch. Fix: move the entire movement group into the bottom-center area (y > 445) within the case silhouette, apply the clip-path to mask it properly inside the case opening, and replace the stray curve with a proper chain of 3-4 links attached to the crown curving upward.

payload.intent: Refine the Movement section to explicitly require defining a clip-path matching the case opening and applying it to the movement group, ensuring strict confinement within the bottom-center area (y > 445). Strengthen the Chain description to mandate distinct oval links attached to the crown stem, curving upward, to replace the previous stray curve and ensure structural correctness.
